title: scurrula thonningii
intro: Scurrula thonningii, also known as the poison tree or poison wood, is a species of flowering plant in the family Clusiaceae. It is traditionally used in West African medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its antimicrobial and antifungal properties, which can help to treat infections and wounds. The health benefits of Scurrula thonningii include its ability to reduce fever and alleviate symptoms associated with malaria, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and act as an anti-inflammatory. In traditional herbal preparations, Scurrula thonningii is often made into a decoction or infusion by steeping the bark, leaves, or roots in hot water to create a tea that can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
